output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 3/3.
Batch 1: T1 completed. I read the agent files, the relevant create-task resolvers, frontend model/default sources, and the harness files, then checkpointed the batch result. Key findings: image generation model routing is `qwen-image`/`qwen-image-2512`/`z-image` -> `qwen_image` or `qwen_image_style` / `qwen_image_2512` / `z_image_turbo`, otherwise default `wan_2_2_t2i`; travel-between-images currently forwards `model_name` verbatim but the app maps UI model IDs `wan-2.2` / `ltx-2.3` / `ltx-2.3-fast` to worker names `wan_2_2_i2v_lightning_baseline_2_2_2` / `ltx2_22B` / `ltx2_22B_distilled` before calling `create-task`; character-animate defaults to `480p`; and `supabase/functions/_tests/harness/diverse-gen-test.ts` exists but only covers 4 current generation cases, not the new families.
Batch 2: Batch 2 completed. T2-T5 are done, and the batch checkpoint was written to `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/expand-the-timeline-agent-s-20260404-0454/execution_batch_2.json`. This batch updated only the agent-side schema, builder/routing, validation/plumbing, and prompt surfaces; full compilation/tests are intentionally deferred to T6.
Batch 3: T6 verified. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed, the discovered `ai-timeline-agent` test modules passed, `npm run test:edge:smoke` passed, the harness file was updated to cover the 5 new generation paths with corrected payload assertions, and a temporary runtime repro script confirmed the new routing/model/lineage behavior. The full edge unit suite still has unrelated failures outside this change surface, and the full harness run is blocked here by external Supabase DNS/network access.

task_updates.executor_notes: Verified all requested source surfaces before any implementation. Resolver-param/default findings: `z_image_turbo_i2i` expects `image_url`, optional `prompt`, `strength`, `enable_prompt_expansion`, `numImages`, `loras`, `shot_id`, `based_on`, `source_variant_id`, `create_as_generation`, `tool_type`; `magic_edit` expects `prompt`, `image_url`, optional `negative_prompt`, `resolution`, `seed`, `in_scene`, `shot_id`, `output_format`, `enable_sync_mode`, `max_wait_seconds`, `enable_base64_output`, `tool_type`, `loras`, `based_on`, `source_variant_id`, `create_as_generation`, `hires_fix`, `qwen_edit_model`, `numImages`; `image_upscale` expects `image_url`, optional `generation_id`, `source_variant_id`, `scale_factor`, `noise_scale`, `output_format`, `shot_id`; `video_enhance` expects `video_url`, `enable_interpolation`, `enable_upscale`, optional `interpolation`, `upscale`, `shot_id`, `based_on`, `source_variant_id`; `character_animate` expects `character_image_url`, `motion_video_url`, optional `prompt`, plus `mode` and `resolution`. FLAG-002 resolved with source evidence: the travel resolver does not map friendly IDs itself, and app callers convert `wan-2.2` / `ltx-2.3` / `ltx-2.3-fast` to worker names before hitting `family: 'travel_between_images'`. FLAG-003 resolved: default character-animate resolution is `480p` in both resolver and frontend defaults. FLAG-004 resolved: `supabase/functions/_tests/harness/diverse-gen-test.ts` exists, but today it only covers 4 generation scenarios and will need expansion later.

Expanded the `create_task` schema to 10 task types total by adding `image-to-image`, `magic-edit`, `image-upscale`, `video-enhance`, and `character-animate`. Added optional `model`, `video_url`, and `strength` properties, broadened the `reference_image_urls` description to all image-input paths, and made `prompt` conditionally described instead of globally required. The model enum uses the T1-confirmed strings `wan-2.2`, `qwen-image`, `z-image`, `ltx-2.3`, and `ltx-2.3-fast`.

Extended `CreateGenerationTaskArgs` with `video_url`, `strength`, `based_on`, and `generation_id`. Added builder functions for `z_image_turbo_i2i`, `magic_edit`, `image_upscale`, `video_enhance`, and `character_animate`, each emitting the resolver-expected keys only: `image_url/prompt/strength/numImages/shot_id/based_on` for img2img, `prompt/image_url/numImages/shot_id/based_on` for magic edit, `image_url/generation_id/shot_id` for upscale, `video_url/enable_interpolation/enable_upscale/shot_id/based_on` for video enhance, and `character_image_url/motion_video_url/mode/resolution/prompt` for character animate. Updated routing to send each task type to its family, mapped friendly travel model IDs to worker model names, and used the confirmed `480p` default for character animate.

Expanded `SUPPORTED_CREATE_TASK_TYPES` to the same 10-value surface as the schema. Prompt validation is now per-type so `image-upscale`, `video-enhance`, and `character-animate` can omit prompts while existing prompt-driven paths still require them. Added validation splits for image references, multi-image travel, and `video_url`, plus `strength` range checking. The tool now derives lineage from selected clips and passes `model_name`, `video_url`, `strength`, `based_on`, and `generation_id` through to `createGenerationTask`, while also including a selected video clip in shot lookup for video-enhance.

Updated the system prompt with a model-selection guide, a task-type guide covering the five new paths, and concrete `create_task` examples for `image-to-image`, `magic-edit`, `image-upscale`, `video-enhance`, and `character-animate`. The examples use the exact schema param names (`model`, `reference_image_urls`, `video_url`, `strength`) and the T1-confirmed model IDs, including `z-image` for text-to-image and `ltx-2.3` for image-to-video.

`npx tsc --noEmit` passed on the final tree. The discovered `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/**/*.test.*` modules all passed when run as full files: `command-parser.test.ts`, `selectedClips.test.ts`, `loop.test.ts`, and `tools/clips.test.ts` passed 18/18 tests. `npm run test:edge:smoke` also passed with 104 passing smoke tests and 8 skipped. I updated `supabase/functions/_tests/harness/diverse-gen-test.ts` so it covers the 5 newly added generation paths plus the new model-selection cases, and corrected its assertions to match the resolver-facing payload keys (`model_name`, `image_url`, `generation_id`, `based_on`). Running the full harness module after that update is blocked in this sandbox before agent execution because Supabase test-user sign-in cannot resolve the external hostname (`getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND ...supabase.co`). To verify the bug fix without network access, I created and ran a short temporary runtime repro script against `createGenerationTask` with stubbed `fetch` and `Deno.env`; it passed for `text-to-image` with `z-image`, `image-to-video` with `ltx-2.3`, and the 5 new task types, confirming correct family routing, model-name mapping, lineage fields (`based_on` / `generation_id`), and `character-animate` default `resolution=480p`. Scoped status/diff confirms no edits under `supabase/functions/create-task/resolvers/`.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. FLAG-002 was resolved from `travelBetweenImages.ts` plus frontend model-mapping sources, FLAG-003 from both resolver and frontend character-animate defaults, and FLAG-004 from the existing harness file and its current test-case list.
Yes. `tool-schemas.ts` now exposes exactly 10 `task_type` values, and the `model` enum uses the T1-confirmed IDs `wan-2.2`, `qwen-image`, `z-image`, `ltx-2.3`, and `ltx-2.3-fast`.
Yes. Each new builder in `generation.ts` was matched against the corresponding resolver contract from T1 and emits the resolver-specific keys rather than a generic payload shape.

Yes. `create-task.ts` now separates prompt-required types, reference-image-required types, `image-to-video`'s 2+ image requirement, and `video_url`-required types without loosening validation for the older prompt-driven paths.
Yes. The prompt examples use the exact schema field names from T2 (`model`, `reference_image_urls`, `video_url`, `strength`) and the exact T1-confirmed model IDs, including `z-image` and `ltx-2.3`.

Partially. TypeScript compiles cleanly, the targeted ai-timeline-agent tests pass, the edge smoke suite passes, and scoped git status/diff confirms no resolver edits with only the expected agent paths plus the harness in scope. The full existing edge unit suite does not fully pass in this worktree because of unrelated failures outside the ai-timeline-agent change surface, and the networked harness run is blocked by sandbox DNS.
